source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Incident at Green Canyon Block 52 Platform A

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Green Canyon Block 52

actions_taken: Operation stopped when stinger latched to boom lace observed
Boom lowered without lowering main block simultaneously
Visual inspection conducted after stinger pulled free
Crane continued in operation until June 6, 2024
BSEE inspectors took crane out of service on June 6, 2024
Independent crane inspector derated crane lifting capacity to 50% on June 10, 2024
Crane boom tip replaced at cost of $70,000
Crane load tested and returned to service on June 16, 2024
event.summary: On May 31, 2024, during boat offloading/backloading operations at Green Canyon Block 52 Platform A, a stinger attached to the crane's main block latched onto a lace of the crane boom. When the PIC instructed the crane operator to lower the boom without simultaneously lowering the main block (ignoring CO-2's warning), the stinger pulled free and broke a weld on the boom lace. Despite discovering the damage the next day during pre-use inspection, the PIC instructed crew to continue using the damaged crane. The crane remained in operation from May 31 until BSEE inspectors took it out of service on June 6, 2024. BSEE was not immediately notified of the incident as required.
key_phrases: stinger hooked to main block latched to lace of crane boom
PIC failed to listen to CO-2 recommendation
stop work authority was not used
failed to identify hazard of leaving stinger on big block
JSA did not cover possibility of stinger hanging up on boom

threats.name: Loose gear (stinger) left attached to main block during lifting operations
Inadequate hazard identification during pre-use inspection
Improper crane operation (lowering boom without lowering main block)

controls.name: Pre-use crane inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detection of loose gear and equipment deficiencies
controls.lod_basis: Frontline operational check before equipment use

supporting_text: A pre-use crane inspection and a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was conducted
Step 6 of the pre-use crane inspection is to visually check for loose gear such as slings, hooks, and shackles
Although a pre-use crane inspection was performed, the stinger attached to the main block was not identified as loose gear

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identification of hazards and operational risks

supporting_text: A pre-use crane inspection and a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was conducted
The JSA did not cover the possibility of the stinger hanging up on the boom
Management systems – The JSA was inadequate. It did not specify the stinger possibly hanging up on boom

controls.name: Crane operator competency and proper operating procedures

supporting_text: The PIC signaled for CO-1 to lower the boom without slacking off the main block
CO-2 advised the PIC to lower the boom and main block at the same time. The PIC ignored the warning continued to signal CO-1 to only lower the boom
The PIC failed to listen and the stinger continued to tighten up until it snapped a weld of the lace freeing itself

controls.name: Stop Work Authority (SWA)
controls.lod_basis: Independent safety intervention by any worker

supporting_text: Also, stop work authority (SWA) was not used when the PIC failed to listen to CO-2 recommendation of lowering the boom and main block at the same time
SWA was not used after the lace on the crane boom was found to be damaged
Communication – Stop Work Authority (SWA) was not used when the boom was lowered without lowering the big block at the same time. SWA was not used after the damaged boom was identified

controls.name: Supervision and Person In Charge (PIC) oversight
controls.barrier_role: Supervisory control and decision-making during operations

supporting_text: The PIC signaled for CO-1 to lower the boom without slacking off the main block. CO-2 advised the PIC to lower the boom and main block at the same time. The PIC ignored the warning
The PIC was notified of the damage to the boom by CO-2. The PIC told the CO-2 to keep using the crane
Supervision – The PIC failed to address the concerns of CO-2 when lowering the boom without lowering the main block

controls.name: Post-incident visual inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detection of equipment damage after incident

supporting_text: The boom was visually inspected for damage by CO-1. No damage was found at this time
The PIC, CO-1, and CO-2 assumed the stinger freed itself and did not observe any damage to the boom
The next morning CO-2 was performing a pre-use inspection of the crane and observed the broken lace

controls.name: Equipment damage reporting and removal from service
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of continued use of damaged equipment

supporting_text: The PIC was notified of the damage to the boom by CO-2. The PIC told the CO-2 to keep using the crane
Next, CO-2 asked about the diminished capacity of the crane and the PIC again told him to keep using the crane as is
The damaged crane stayed in operation from May 31, 2024, until June 6, 2024, when the Inspectors took the crane out of service
Instead, CO-1 and CO-2 continued to run the crane with a damaged boom
BSEE Houma District was not orally notified of this incident
This incident was uncovered on an inspection conducted by BSEE Houma District inspectors on June 6, 2024
At the time of inspection, it was discovered that a crane incident occurred on May 31st, 2024, BSEE was not immediately notified following the incident

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
